title: Getting started with daos
text: Reading set files base R tidyverse requires pipeline list.files(), lapply() purrr::map(), manual naming. statistician want one function one thing. read_files() handles path expansion, existence checks, format detection, naming, collection single call. single path returns object directly; multiple paths return named list progress bar. Supported formats: csv, tsv, parquet, feather, xlsx, xls, rds, sas7bdat, sav, por, xpt, dta, json, ndjson, jsonl, yaml, yml, txt. practice, read_files() works best data production consistent – formats, column names, types across files. read_files() handles set files without warnings, workflow order. warns type mismatches, signal something changed upstream. Supply custom reader override auto-detection add arguments: Column name casing default, column names converted lowercase reading (.lowercase = TRUE). Set .lowercase = FALSE preserve original casing. Binding one tibble Set = "bind" row-bind files. Use .id track origin row. names numeric vector (e.g. years), .id column also numeric. column types differ across files, warning issued types reconciled readr::type_convert(). .id column always excluded reconciliation. Unpacking individual variables Set = "unpack" assign file named variable calling environment. default, read_files() aborts name already exists – set .overwrite = TRUE allow overwriting.
code: # Single file (returns object directly): df <- read_files("data/results.parquet")  # Multiple files with glue expansion: lst <- read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = 0:9) read_files(   "data/dat{0:9}.parquet",   reader = \(x) arrow::read_parquet(x, col_select = 1:5) ) read_files(   "data/dat{2020:2024}.parquet",   names = 2020:2024,   out   = "bind",   .id   = "year" ) read_files("data/dat{0:9}.parquet", names = paste0("dat", 0:9), out = "unpack")

title: Getting started with daos

code: mget(ls(pattern = "dat\\d"))   # base R summon("dat\\d")               # daos dat1 <- data.frame(x = 1) dat2 <- data.frame(x = 2) dat3 <- data.frame(x = 3)  result <- summon("^dat\\d+$") names(result) #> [1] "dat1" "dat2" "dat3"

what: read_access() – read from a Microsoft Access database
title: Getting started with daos
text: Connects .mdb .accdb file via ODBC, executes SQL query, returns result tibble. Requires DBI odbc packages Microsoft Access ODBC driver. verbosity argument controls output: "compact" (default) – one line per file, best looping "full" – header, spinners, summary; best single files "quiet" – output
code: # Full output for interactive use: df <- read_access("data/sales.mdb", "SELECT * FROM Customers",                   verbosity = "full")  # Loop over many databases quietly: files <- fs::dir_ls("data", glob = "*.mdb") results <- purrr::map(files, \(f) read_access(f, "SELECT * FROM Sales"))

title: Getting started with daos

code: df <- read_xbrl("report.xml") df <- read_xbrl("report.xml", encoding = "latin1")

what: accounts_pdf_to_txt() – convert PDF accounts to text
title: Getting started with daos
text: Reads PDF files directory, extracts text, writes one .txt file per PDF. Intended first step manual review workflow financial statements. Requires pdftools. output directory created automatically exist. File names (typically CVR numbers) preserved.
code: accounts_pdf_to_txt(   pdf_dir = "data/pdf",   txt_dir = "data/txt" )

what: accounts_txt_to_xlsx() – parse accounts text files to Excel
title: Getting started with daos
text: Parses manually formatted text files exports result Excel file. Requires writexl. Text file format line either category line data line: Category line: single string field delimiter. Sets note value subsequent data lines. amount year - 1. Amounts must whole kroner; periods used thousands separators stripped automatically. previous-year amount exists, third field may empty – becomes NA. Appending statnatio category line negates amounts category, useful costs appear positive sign notes. suffix stripped final output. File names used identifiers cvr column. trailing _spec suffix stripped automatically. Three validation checks run automatically abort failure: Comma value columns – indicates wrong decimal separator NA note elementid – indicates missing category line NA current-year values – indicates parsing failure
code: df <- accounts_txt_to_xlsx(   txt_dir  = "data/txt",   out_file = "data/output.xlsx",   year     = 2024 )

what: expect_empty() – assert a data frame is empty
title: Getting started with daos
text: Often want assert filtering condition finds nothing – find something, want know immediately clear message. Writing custom (nrow(x) > 0) cli::cli_abort(...) every time repetitive. expect_empty() one step, fits pipeline, optionally writes log file. filtering impossibilities piping expect_empty(), offending rows hand moment something goes wrong. log argument writes timestamped entry file. eventually open RStudio investigate, errors already collected:
code: # Success: dplyr::starwars |>   filter(name == "Harry Potter") |>   expect_empty(     success_msg = "No Harry Potter rows in starwars",     warn_msg    = "Unexpected fictional character found"   ) #> ✔ No Harry Potter rows in starwars # Warning -- rows found: dplyr::starwars |>   filter(mass > 1000) |>   expect_empty(warn_msg = "Unrealistic mass values") #> Warning: Unrealistic mass values # Hard abort: dplyr::starwars |>   filter(mass > 1000) |>   expect_empty(abort_msg = "Unrealistic mass values") log_path <- f("log/{nowf()}/checks.log") checker  <- purrr::partial(expect_empty, log = log_path)  dplyr::starwars |>   filter(name == "Harry Potter") |>   checker(success_msg = "No fictional characters")  dplyr::starwars |>   filter(height > 250) |>   checker(warn_msg = "Unusually tall characters")

what: flag_duplicates() – detect and label duplicate rows
title: Getting started with daos
text: Base R’s duplicated() marks second occurrence duplicate. flag_duplicates() marks copies assigns shared group ID – making easy inspect instances . ID can also serve implicit record linkage key: multiple respondents reported information background data missing one person, shared dupid reveals connection. Combine expect_empty() pipeline validation gate:
code: flag_duplicates(ggplot2::mpg) #> # A tibble: 234 × 13 #>    isdup dupid manufacturer model      displ  year   cyl trans drv     cty   hwy #>    <lgl> <int> <chr>        <chr>      <dbl> <int> <int> <chr> <chr> <int> <int> #>  1 FALSE     0 audi         a4           1.8  1999     4 auto… f        18    29 #>  2 FALSE     0 audi         a4           1.8  1999     4 manu… f        21    29 #>  3 FALSE     0 audi         a4           2    2008     4 manu… f        20    31 #>  4 FALSE     0 audi         a4           2    2008     4 auto… f        21    30 #>  5 FALSE     0 audi         a4           2.8  1999     6 auto… f        16    26 #>  6 FALSE     0 audi         a4           2.8  1999     6 manu… f        18    26 #>  7 FALSE     0 audi         a4           3.1  2008     6 auto… f        18    27 #>  8 FALSE     0 audi         a4 quattro   1.8  1999     4 manu… 4        18    26 #>  9 FALSE     0 audi         a4 quattro   1.8  1999     4 auto… 4        16    25 #> 10 FALSE     0 audi         a4 quattro   2    2008     4 manu… 4        20    28 #> # ℹ 224 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: fl <chr>, class <chr> # Check specific columns: flag_duplicates(ggplot2::mpg, manufacturer, model, year) |>   filter(isdup) |>   arrange(dupid) #> # A tibble: 227 × 13 #>    isdup dupid manufacturer model      displ  year   cyl trans drv     cty   hwy #>    <lgl> <int> <chr>        <chr>      <dbl> <int> <int> <chr> <chr> <int> <int> #>  1 TRUE      1 audi         a4           1.8  1999     4 auto… f        18    29 #>  2 TRUE      1 audi         a4           1.8  1999     4 manu… f        21    29 #>  3 TRUE      1 audi         a4           2.8  1999     6 auto… f        16    26 #>  4 TRUE      1 audi         a4           2.8  1999     6 manu… f        18    26 #>  5 TRUE      2 audi         a4           2    2008     4 manu… f        20    31 #>  6 TRUE      2 audi         a4           2    2008     4 auto… f        21    30 #>  7 TRUE      2 audi         a4           3.1  2008     6 auto… f        18    27 #>  8 TRUE      3 audi         a4 quattro   1.8  1999     4 manu… 4        18    26 #>  9 TRUE      3 audi         a4 quattro   1.8  1999     4 auto… 4        16    25 #> 10 TRUE      3 audi         a4 quattro   2.8  1999     6 auto… 4        15    25 #> # ℹ 217 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: fl <chr>, class <chr> ggplot2::mpg |>   flag_duplicates() |>   filter(isdup) |>   expect_empty(warn_msg = "Duplicate rows detected") #> Warning: Duplicate rows detected

what: split_by() – split a data frame into a named list

code: parts <- split_by(ggplot2::mpg, manufacturer) names(parts)[1:5] #> [1] "audi"      "chevrolet" "dodge"     "ford"      "honda" head(parts[["audi"]]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   manufacturer model displ  year   cyl trans      drv     cty   hwy fl    class  #>   <chr>        <chr> <dbl> <int> <int> <chr>      <chr> <int> <int> <chr> <chr>  #> 1 audi         a4      1.8  1999     4 auto(l5)   f        18    29 p     compa… #> 2 audi         a4      1.8  1999     4 manual(m5) f        21    29 p     compa… #> 3 audi         a4      2    2008     4 manual(m6) f        20    31 p     compa… #> 4 audi         a4      2    2008     4 auto(av)   f        21    30 p     compa… #> 5 audi         a4      2.8  1999     6 auto(l5)   f        16    26 p     compa… #> 6 audi         a4      2.8  1999     6 manual(m5) f        18    26 p     compa… parts2 <- split_by(ggplot2::mpg, cyl, drv, .sep = "-") names(parts2)[1:4] #> [1] "4-4" "4-f" "5-f" "6-4"

title: Getting started with daos
text: Many people find hard get close enough data working console – Excel’s immediate, visual feedback difficult replicate. screen_timeseries() attempt bridge gap: launches Shiny app navigate time series group group, zoom , flag anomalies – without writing filtering code. columns x, y, series, excluded automatically become dropdown filters. Requires shiny highcharter. Keyboard shortcuts .y_min .y_max arguments fix Y-axis bounds globally across groups. .title argument sets title shown app header downloaded figures:
code: df <- expand.grid(   year    = 2010:2020,   country = c("DK", "SE", "NO", "FI") ) df$gdp <- rnorm(nrow(df), mean = 300, sd = 30)  screen_timeseries(df, x = year, y = gdp)  # Multiple lines per group with series: ggplot2::economics_long |>   screen_timeseries(date, value, series = variable) screen_timeseries(df, year, gdp,                   .y_min = 0, .y_max = 500,                   .title = "GDP by country")

title: Parse formatted text files and export to Excel — accounts_txt_to_xlsx
text: Text file format: line either category line data line: Category line: single string field delimiter. Becomes note column subsequent data lines. title: Extract information from Danish CPR numbers — cpr_info
text: data tibble data frame. cpr_col Name CPR column (unquoted). add info types add. Either: unnamed character vector, e.g. c("bday", "age") — uses type names column names. named character vector, e.g. c(birth_date = "bday", years_old = "age") — left-hand side becomes column name, right-hand side type. Default: six types. ref_date Reference date age calculation. Accepts Date object ISO-format string ("YYYY-MM-DD"). Default: Sys.Date().

title: Extract information from Danish CPR numbers — cpr_info
text: Supported info types (add values): Century detection follows official CPR Register rules based digit 7 two-digit year component. Input tolerance: dashes spaces stripped automatically nine-digit numbers zero-padded left (recovering values lost leading zero Excel). CPR column returned data frame always returned standardised 10-digit format xxxxxxxxxx.

what: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: Searches environment objects whose names match regular expression returns named list. Useful collecting family similarly-named objects (e.g. dat0 dat9) unpacking list = "unpack" read_files().

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
code: summon(pattern, .envir = parent.frame())

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: pattern single regular expression string. .envir environment search. Default calling environment.

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
text: named list matching objects.

title: Retrieve objects matching a pattern from an environment — summon
code: dat1 <- data.frame(x = 1) dat2 <- data.frame(x = 2) dat3 <- data.frame(x = 3) summon("^dat\\d+$") #> $dat1 #>   x #> 1 1 #>  #> $dat2 #>   x #> 1 2 #>  #> $dat3 #>   x #> 1 3 #>

what: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
text: Installs task callback automatically saves data frame returned console named variable global environment. Intermediate expressions (1 + 1, plots, etc.) ignored — top-level data frame returns captured.

title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
code: track_last_df(on = TRUE, name = ".last.df")

title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
text: TRUE enable tracking (default), FALSE disable. name Name variable written .GlobalEnv. Default: ".last.df".

title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
text: TRUE invisibly.

title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
text: Calling track_last_df() replaces existing callback, preventing duplicates.

title: Track the last data frame printed in the console — track_last_df
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ track_last_df()          # enable dplyr::starwars |> head() .last.df                 # the last printed data frame  track_last_df(FALSE)     # disable } # }

what: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: Shows column types one data frames side--side. Useful diagnosing type mismatches join binding read_files().

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
code: view_types(..., diff = FALSE, focus = NULL)

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: ... Data frames compare (unquoted). Names inferred expressions passed. diff Logical. TRUE, columns types differ across datasets shown. Default: FALSE. focus named length-1 character vector, e.g. c(year = "int"). supplied, named column shown, datasets type match expected type. Returns empty tibble (0 rows) types match — suitable use tests.

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
text: tibble column column one column per input dataset showing pillar::type_sum() type string.

title: Compare column types across data frames — view_types
code: df1 <- data.frame(x = 1L, y = "a") df2 <- data.frame(x = 1.0, y = "b")  view_types(df1, df2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   column df1   df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> <chr> #> 1 x      int   dbl   #> 2 y      chr   chr   view_types(df1, df2, diff = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   column df1   df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> <chr> #> 1 x      int   dbl   view_types(df1, df2, focus = c(x = "int")) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   column df2   #>   <chr>  <chr> #> 1 x      dbl
